title: Pleurothallis ascendens
intro: Pleurothallis ascendens is a species of orchid, belonging to the family Orchidaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which are said to aid in wound healing and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Pleurothallis ascendens include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which have been identified as responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory problems and to promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Pleurothallis ascendens is classified in the family Orchidaceae and within the subfamily Epidendroideae. The genus Pleurothallis is a part of the tribe Cymbidieae. This classification is based on the plant's physical characteristics and its evolutionary relationships with other orchids. The family Orchidaceae is the largest and most diverse group of flowering plants. Further classification of Pleurothallis ascendens is also made at the species and infra-specific levels.
